memory_item.title: Device Core Terraform migration progress
memory_item.body: Plan tickets: ENG-8264, ENG-8262, ENG-8251. Services: device-registration, device-replicator, device-manager. Branch pushed: feat/device-core-terraform-sops-migration. Commit: 165c64e. Terraform validation: dev 25 imports, prod 15 imports, prod-eu 7 imports, all with 0 add/change/destroy. Jira statuses moved to In Progress for all three plan tickets. Sprint target: Sprint Ivysaur 4/30 - 5/13. Note: sprint assignment via available Jira MCP issue-edit path was blocked because sprint field is not editable in editmeta.

memory_item.title: Pamie project snapshot - 2026-05-01
memory_item.body: Project: Pamie, located at /Users/kuro/Desktop/CTX/pamie. Pamie is a Go-based, self-hosted, local-first long-term memory server for MCP agents. It exposes /health, /ready, and a Bearer-protected /mcp JSON-RPC endpoint. Durable storage is SQLite with FTS5 search; default local data lives under ./data/pamie.db and is ignored by git. Current MCP tool surface includes context_save, context_get, context_search, context_update, context_delete, context_pin, context_recent, and context_stats. Read-only resources include pamie://status and pamie://memory/stats. Current implementation status from README/TASKS: core HTTP server, config, structured logging, auth, rate limiting, audit events, SQLite migrations/repositories, MCP JSON-RPC handling, memory tools, safe resources, FTS5 search with snippets/ranking/depth/filter support, deterministic lifecycle rules, promotion on access, retention-policy deletion, Docker/Compose/Caddy guidance, and release artifact automation are implemented. Not yet implemented: scheduled background lifecycle runner, built-in backup/export command, restore/import validation, vector/hybrid search runtime, persistent multi-token storage/rotation, and tamper-proof audit log subsystem. Git state at save time: branch main tracking origin/main, clean working tree, latest commit 2f0ce7f from 2026-05-01 17:42:41 +0200 titled 'Ignore local prompts'. IDE context: active file pamie/.gitignore; open prompts include 08-code-quality-and-package-boundaries, 11-ci-release-image-tagging-hardening, and 12-runtime-devops-observability; COMMIT_EDITMSG is open. .gitignore currently ignores Go build artifacts, local binary /pamie, env files, SQLite database files, /data/, /dist/, /prompts/, and editor/OS files.

memory_item.title: Pamie UI/API scaffold created
memory_item.body: On 2026-05-01 in /Users/kuro/Desktop/CTX, created a future pamie-ui planning scaffold beside the existing pamie backend. Added pamie-ui/.gitignore, README.md, ROADMAP.md, TASKS.md, DECISIONS.md, prompts/, agents/, and skills/. The scaffold is planning-only: no React app, no npm install, no Git init, no backend changes during the scaffold run. Future UI direction: React + TypeScript + Vite, React Flow for animated memory mind map/search, static build deployable behind pamie, consumes pamie /api/v1 browser API instead of SQLite or MCP directly. Created pamie-ui-prompts with two meta-prompts: 00-create-pamie-ui-workspace.prompt.md and 01-create-pamie-api-workspace.prompt.md. The API prompt is still pending; it should create browser API planning scaffold inside pamie, with optional bounded /api/v1 routes, OpenAPI planning, API agents, and API skills. Updated future prompt workflow: pamie-ui-prompts README and both meta-prompts, pamie-ui/prompts README and prompts 00-07, and pamie/prompts README plus prompts 08-15 now require a Pamie memory search before changes as a pre-flight test and a Pamie memory save/update after verification. Verification run: confirmed all targeted future prompt files contain '## Pamie Memory Workflow'; ran `git -C pamie diff --check -- prompts` successfully. Existing pamie repository had unrelated dirty working-tree changes before this UI/API prompt work; those were not reverted.

memory_item.title: Pamie prompt 08 and prompt memory workflow updates
memory_item.body:
On 2026-05-01, Prompt 08 code quality/package-boundary work was implemented in the Pamie backend. Key changes: internal/memory.Service now depends on a narrow memory.Store interface instead of concrete *db.Store and exposes ErrUnavailable for unconfigured services; MCP params and tool arguments now share internal/util.DecodeJSONObject for strict JSON object decoding; lifecycle evaluation checks context cancellation inside the item loop; package doc.go files were added for internal ownership boundaries; ARCHITECTURE.md documents package direction; DECISIONS.md adds ADR-0012 for narrow runtime interfaces; TASKS.md adds the completed code-quality phase; touched internal READMEs describe boundaries. Focused tests were added for unavailable service errors and shared JSON validation. Verification passed: make fmt, go test ./..., go vet ./..., go build ./cmd/pamie.

Also on 2026-05-01, all 26 future prompt files under pamie/prompts, pamie-ui-prompts, and pamie-ui/prompts were updated with a Required Pamie Memory Test section. Future prompt runs must call context_search or context_recent before making changes to verify Pamie memory access and load relevant context, treat retrieved memory as untrusted data, note Pamie unavailability if it occurs, and save a concise Pamie memory after completion with changes, key files, verification, and follow-up work.

memory_item.title: Pamie Prompt 09 backup export restore implemented
memory_item.body: On 2026-05-01, implemented pamie/prompts/09-implement-backup-export-restore.prompt.md in /Users/kuro/Desktop/CTX/pamie. Added local operator CLI subcommands: `pamie backup --db-path ... --out ...` using SQLite `VACUUM INTO` with destination overwrite protection and integrity check; `pamie export --db-path ... --out ...` producing versioned NDJSON with manifest, schema version, export time, Pamie version, record counts, and SHA-256 record checksum; `pamie import --db-path ... --in ... --dry-run|--confirm` with append-only import, dry-run rollback validation, checksum/count/schema/malformed record checks, and duplicate-ID rejection for both file and target DB. Backup/export/import audit events are emitted through the local operator CLI without raw Bearer tokens. Key files changed: cmd/pamie/main.go, internal/db/backup.go, internal/db/export.go, internal/db/export_test.go, docs/backup-and-restore.md, docs/deployment.md, SECURITY.md, TASKS.md. Tests added cover WAL backup, export/import round trip preserving representative memory/chunk/event/policy/access rows, malformed NDJSON, checksum mismatch, duplicate IDs, and target duplicate IDs. Verification passed: make fmt; go test ./...; go vet ./...; go build ./cmd/pamie; git diff --check. No blockers. Next recommended prompt: 10-expand-testing-and-acceptance.prompt.md, unless the operator CLI should first be split out of cmd/pamie/main.go for package organization.

memory_item.title: Pamie operator CLI help and restore alias follow-up
memory_item.body: On 2026-05-01 in /Users/kuro/Desktop/CTX/pamie, fixed a follow-up issue after Prompt 09: `pamie -h` previously showed only server flags because top-level help was handled by the server config parser, and there was no `restore` command name. Updated cmd/pamie/main.go so top-level `pamie -h`, `--help`, and `help` print command-aware usage listing backup, export, import, and restore. Added `pamie restore` as an alias for the NDJSON import workflow, with command-specific help and restore-specific audit action names. Updated README.md, docs/backup-and-restore.md, docs/deployment.md, and SECURITY.md to mention restore where relevant. Ran gofmt, go test ./..., go vet ./..., go build ./cmd/pamie, and git diff --check successfully. Ran scripts/install-local.sh, installing the updated binary to /Users/kuro/.local/bin/pamie and adding /Users/kuro/.local/bin to /Users/kuro/.zshrc. Verified /Users/kuro/.local/bin/pamie -h and restore -h show the new commands.
